critic_raw:
ASSESSOR 1:
Verdict: empirical_test_agreed

Three issues are present: (1) GNN threshold is validation-optimized for 0.8% review rate; rules engine threshold is a legacy setting that accidentally hits 0.8% due to seasonal volume reduction — asymmetric operating point optimization; (2) analyst-revised rule weights (4 months before study) were not replayed — the rules engine is running stale weights; (3) single-month evaluation with seasonally low volume is insufficient for the headcount reallocation claim. Issues 1 and 2 are empirically testable. The GNN advantage direction is plausible but magnitude uncertain under fair comparison.
defender_raw:
ASSESSOR 2:
Verdict: empirical_test_agreed

The operating point mismatch is real — GNN was tuned for the 0.8% budget; the rules engine hit it by accident. This is an important asymmetry. The stale rules snapshot weakens the comparison further. Both issues can be resolved by re-running with updated weights at an explicitly calibrated threshold. The direction of GNN advantage is likely to persist but may narrow.

ASSESSOR 3:
Verdict: empirical_test_agreed

All three issues are present and well-supported. The operating point mismatch is the clearest methodological problem — you cannot compare a tuned-to-budget model against an accidentally-on-budget model and claim fair comparison. The stale weights add to the weakness. An empirical test with updated rules at matched threshold is the right resolution. The GNN may still win; that's what the test determines.
adjudication_raw: All three assessors independently agree: empirical_test_agreed. Issues are genuine and the direction of advantage is plausible but magnitude uncertain. ETD constraint: measure = fraud capture rate at 0.8% review rate on same test month, with updated rules weights and explicitly-calibrated threshold. Success criterion: GNN leads by ≥ 8 pp. Failure criterion: gap narrows below 5 pp.

empirical_test.measure: Fraud capture rate at 0.8% review-rate threshold on the same test month
empirical_test.success_criterion: GNN fraud capture rate ≥ 70% vs. updated rules engine (revised weights, explicitly-designed 0.8% threshold) ≤ 62%, confirming genuine GNN advantage
empirical_test.failure_criterion: Gap between GNN and updated rules engine narrows below 5 pp, indicating the advantage was primarily operating point and staleness artifacts
